segments.text: Seriously, how can someone make such a big mistake in logo design that it becomes legendary for all the wrong reasons? Kicking us at number three, we
have Mega Flick. This video rental store wanted a fun, familyfriendly logo, but they forgot about the letter spacing in
the word flick, where the letter L and I got way too close for comfort. This logo had such a tight spacing that people
thought they were renting something else, not the kind of place where you would like to take your kids on a typical Friday night. Coming in at
number two, we have Arlington Pediatric Center. A place meant to care for children. The logo featured abstract human figures. It instead created a
imaginary that many found shockingly inappropriate at a first glance. Taking the spot at the first place. It's a
legendary kids exchange fail. They wanted to promote their kids consignment store, but the poor kerning made the name sound completely else. So remember
folks, a great logo is worth a thousand words. But sometime those thousand words can be
